metadata.title: TRACE Negative Control — Routine Meeting Time v0.1
metadata.status: negative control case graph; not validation; expected to add no decision-relevant delta
metadata.purpose: Test whether TRACE can idle on a routine low-stakes scheduling choice with no serious harm clock, no protected-scope collapse, no authority asymmetry, and no correction-channel capture.

case_input.situation: A small peer group needs to choose a routine meeting time from mutually available options. No participant faces a material access barrier, safety risk, deadline hardening, employment penalty, medical/care obligation, or meaningful authority asymmetry under the stated facts.
known_uncertainty: case is deliberately low-stakes and stylized
if hidden care duties, disability access constraints, employment coercion, or deadline pressure appear, this is no longer a negative control
TRACE should not manufacture major laundering or correction claims from the stated facts

claims.statement: Under the stated facts, TRACE is expected to add no material decision-relevant delta over a competent baseline scheduling analysis; its machinery should largely idle.

evidence_basis.basis_summary: This is a deliberately mundane negative control constructed to test whether TRACE over-patterns low-stakes decisions.

unknowns: whether hidden constraints exist outside stated facts

affected_scopes.scope_role: ordinary participants with reversible schedule preferences

contest_edge.route: participants can reply, decline, suggest alternatives, or cancel the meeting
contest_edge.time_limit: before calendar invite is accepted or meeting occurs

claims.notes: If TRACE generates strong harm, laundering, or authority claims from this stated case, that is evidence of pattern overfit.

clocks.hardening: minor inconvenience only; reversible by reply or reschedule under stated facts

transitions.from_state: multiple mutually workable meeting times exist
to_state_set: participants retain ordinary ability to contest or reschedule
clock_effect: soft preference narrows but does not materially harden harm under stated facts
transitions.review_debt: none beyond ordinary rescheduling if a participant objects

correction_paths.route: reply to thread or calendar invite with objection or alternative
correction_paths.failure_mode: only fails if hidden authority pressure or access constraint exists, which would change the case type

evaluator_stack.role: case builder testing whether TRACE can idle
evaluator_stack.capture_risk: medium because negative control may be under-specified
evaluator_stack.contestability: high; any hidden serious constraint should reclassify the case

baseline_falsifier.question: Would a competent baseline scheduling analysis identify the same practical next action without TRACE machinery?
answer: Yes: choose a mutually available time or ask for updated availability; no special harm/correction machinery should be needed under the stated facts.
TRACE_added_value_claim: Expected added value is near-zero; the graph is useful only as a negative control against over-patterning.

open_wounds: If hidden care duties, disability access needs, job coercion, major deadline pressure, or power asymmetry appear, this case must be reclassified and is no longer a negative control.
Run validator after creation; if schema-valid, run baseline-vs-TRACE comparison to check whether TRACE improperly manufactures deltas.
next_required_build: run baseline vs TRACE negative-control comparison
if TRACE creates major deltas here, record pattern-overfit failure
